verdict_reasoning: KLK2 is a secreted serine protease that lacks transmembrane domains, GPI anchors, or other intrinsic membrane-anchoring mechanisms. Although recent studies demonstrate KLK2 protein can be detected near prostate cancer cell surfaces and is targetable by antibodies and CAR-T therapeutics, this surface association reflects extracellular accumulation of a secreted protein rather than stable surface localization via the protein's own mechanism. The protein body does not reach the outer membrane leaflet through its own anchor and would not be wash-resistant. Therapeutic targeting of secreted extracellular protein does not confer surface accessibility status.
